statement.markdown:
Given an integer n, return all distinct solutions to the n-queens puzzle.
   Place n queens on an n x n chessboard such that no two queens attack each other.
statement.inputFormat: Input is an integer n, where 1 <= n <= 8.
statement.outputFormat: Output should be a list of list of integers, representing the positions of the queens on the board for each solution.
statement.notes: Note that an attacker cell is any cell that is occupied by a queen or any cell that shares the same row or column as a queen. Also, the input n will be within the valid range.

hints.text: Think about how you can use backtracking to find all distinct solutions to the n-queens puzzle.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: vector<vector<int>> solveNQueens(int n) { vector<vector<int>> res; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { res.push_back(vector<int>()); for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) { res[i].push_back(0); } } return res; }}
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution {public List<List<Integer>> solveNQueens(int n) { List<List<Integer>> res = new ArrayList<>(); for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { List<Integer> tmp = new ArrayList<>(); for (int j = 0; j < n; j++) { tmp.add(0); } res.add(tmp); } return res; }}
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solveNQueens(self, n: int) -> List[List[int]]: res = []; for i in range(n): tmp = []; for j in range(n): tmp.append(0); res.append(tmp); return res
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solveNQueens(n) { var res = []; for (var i = 0; i < n; i++) { var tmp = []; for (var j = 0; j < n; j++) { tmp.push(0); } res.push(tmp); } return res;}

solutions.python: class Solution: def solveNQueens(self, n: int) -> List[List[int]]: res = []; for i in range(n): tmp = []; for j in range(n): tmp.append(0); res.append(tmp); if i < n - 1: res.extend(self.solveNQueens(n - 1)); return res
solutions.cpp: class Solution {public: vector<vector<int>> solveNQueens(int n) { int res = solveUtil(0,n); return res; } vector<vector<int>> solveUtil(int col,int n,vector<int>& places) { if (col == n) { vector<vector<int>> res; res.push_back(places); return res; } vector<vector<int>> res; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { if (!isPlaceValid(col, i, places)) { continue; } places.push_back(i); vector<vector<int>> newRes = solveUtil(col + 1, n, places); for (auto &r : newRes) { r.push_back(places.back()); } places.pop_back(); if (newRes.empty()) { res.push_back(places); } res.insert(res.end(), newRes.begin(), newRes.end()); } return res; } bool isPlaceValid(int col,int row,vector<int>& places) { int j = 0; for (int i = 0; i < places.size(); i++) { if (places[i] == row || places[i] - i == row - j || places[i] + i == row + j) { return false; } j++; } return true; }}
